summary: Completed morning outreach research. Found 5 Twitter/X posts to engage with, identified 2 frustrated users for DMs, and checked Discord communities for support opportunities.

key_findings: Issue #32176: Discord bot can't receive messages

tweet_opportunities.author: @matthewjetthall
tweet_opportunities.issue: NODE22 OpenClaw - spent 14 hours troubleshooting, Node version issues with Telegram
tweet_opportunities.reply_tip: Node 22 is required for latest OpenClaw. Try `node --version` to verify. If stuck on Node 20, use nvm to switch: `nvm install 22 && nvm use 22`. Also check Telegram bot token hasn't expired.
tweet_opportunities.dm_note: High frustration, spent 14 hours. Good candidate for personalized help.

tweet_opportunities.author: @xunxuntj
tweet_opportunities.issue: OpenClaw agent hit 429 error, 106-hour timeout, burned through API quota
tweet_opportunities.reply_tip: Rate limits are tough! Add `rateLimit: { requestsPerMinute: 10 }` to your agent config. For 429s, implement exponential backoff. Consider local models (mlx-local/llama-3.2-1b) for non-critical tasks.
tweet_opportunities.dm_note: Very frustrated but has sense of humor. Good engagement opportunity.

tweet_opportunities.author: @bilbeny
tweet_opportunities.issue: 2026.2.19 upgrade broke all tool connections - infinite pairing loop
tweet_opportunities.reply_tip: Known issue with 2026.2.19! New scopes (operator.write/read) weren't auto-granted. Fix: `openclaw devices rotate --device <id> --scope operator.admin --scope operator.write --scope operator.read`. See GitHub issue #23006 for full workaround.
tweet_opportunities.dm_note: Already shared solution publicly, technical user.

tweet_opportunities.author: @StMichaelsForge
tweet_opportunities.issue: All configured models failed to respond error
tweet_opportunities.reply_tip: 'All models failed' usually means API key issues or rate limits. Check: 1) `openclaw doctor` for key validity 2) Try a simple model first: `model: local/mlx-local/llama-3.2-1b` 3) Check gateway logs: `openclaw logs --follow`
tweet_opportunities.dm_note: Already getting help from Grok, less urgent.

tweet_opportunities.author: @herbertyang
tweet_opportunities.issue: Ghost cron jobs - deleted jobs keep running after gateway restart
tweet_opportunities.reply_tip: Great catch! The .tmp recovery files are the culprit. Quick fix: `rm ~/.openclaw/cron/*.tmp` then restart gateway. For permanent fix, follow GitHub issue #6814 you already filed.
tweet_opportunities.dm_note: Technical user, already filed GitHub issue.

dm_targets.pain_point: Spent 14 hours troubleshooting Node version issues with OpenClaw + Telegram
dm_targets.approach: Empathetic, direct solution. Offer 1:1 debugging session.
dm_targets.draft_message: Hey! Saw your tweet about the 14-hour Node 22 struggle - that's rough. The Node version check in OpenClaw is strict but the error messaging could be clearer. Happy to jump on a quick call to walk through your setup and get you unblocked. We've helped folks through this exact issue. DM me if interested! — OpenClaw Debugger Team

dm_targets.pain_point: Burned through API quota, facing 106-hour timeout, frustrated but humorous about it
dm_targets.approach: Match their humor, provide immediate actionable tips + offer deeper help.
dm_targets.draft_message: That 106-hour timeout is a special kind of pain 😅 Your agent really did go full buffet mode. Quick wins: 1) Add rate limiting to your config 2) Use local models for dev/testing (mlx-local is solid) 3) Implement token budgeting. Want help setting up guardrails so this doesn't happen again? We're here! — OpenClaw Debugger Team

recent_issues_found.solution: Restart OpenClaw gateway via menubar or `openclaw gateway restart`

recent_issues_found.issue: Discord plugin not available after fresh install
recent_issues_found.solution: Enable plugins in Gateway Dashboard > Settings > Plugin entries

recent_issues_found.root_cause: Memory crisis - compaction mode set to 'safeguard' instead of 'default'
recent_issues_found.solution: Change compaction mode to 'default', restart gateway

recent_issues_found.issue: Bot online but not receiving messages
recent_issues_found.root_cause: Missing privileged intents or health-monitor restarts due to 'stuck' state
recent_issues_found.solution: Enable all privileged intents in Discord bot settings

discord_findings.engagement_opportunity: Active support threads on AnswerOverflow with users posting 'plugin not available' issues. Could monitor and provide proactive debugging help.
discord_findings.recommended_action: Join OpenClaw Discord, monitor #help channel, offer debugging assistance to users with complex issues that aren't getting responses.

reddit_findings.issue: Returns only 'U' when chatting, API key set up correctly
reddit_findings.possible_cause: SSL verification issues or Node version mismatch
reddit_findings.workaround_found: Temporarily disable SSL verification via NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED=0 (not recommended for production)

reddit_findings.post: Discord and other channels show 'plugin not available' after fresh install
reddit_findings.issue: Plugins show as installed but not working, can't enable them
reddit_findings.solution: Go to Gateway Dashboard > Settings > Plugin entries > Enable > Save

github_issues_tracked.title: 2026.2.19 upgrade breaks tool connections
github_issues_tracked.impact: All users upgrading from 2026.2.15 to 2026.2.19+ experiencing infinite pairing loop

github_issues_tracked.title: Discord bot can't receive messages
github_issues_tracked.impact: Discord bots online but not receiving messages due to stuck state

github_issues_tracked.title: Ghost cron jobs after deletion
github_issues_tracked.impact: Deleted cron jobs keep running due to .tmp file recovery logic

Create saved response templates for common issues (plugin not available, Node version, rate limiting)
Follow up on GitHub issue #23006 - many users affected by upgrade breaking changes
